title: 11 Best Herbal Teas For Jaw Clenching
intro: Herbal teas for jaw clenching, also known as bruxism, are a natural remedy that involves drinking tea made from various herbs to alleviate the symptoms of this common condition. These herbal teas can treat jaw clenching by relaxing the muscles, reducing stress and anxiety, and promoting a good night's sleep, all of which are contributing factors to bruxism. Examples of herbal teas that can help with jaw clenching include chamomile tea, which calms the nervous system and promotes relaxation, lavender tea, which reduces stress and anxiety, peppermint tea, which eases muscle tension, and passionflower tea, which promotes deep relaxation and sleep. Additionally, other herbal teas such as valerian root tea, lemon balm tea, and ginger tea can also help to alleviate the symptoms of jaw clenching by reducing inflammation, promoting relaxation, and calming the nervous system.

preparations.herb_desc: Valeriana officinalis teas, also known as valerian root tea, have been traditionally used to treat the jaw clenching ailment, also known as bruxism. The relaxing properties of this herbal preparation help to treat this ailment by promoting relaxation and reducing muscle tension, leading to a decrease in jaw clenching episodes. The bioactive constituents, including valerenic acid, valeranone, and isovalerenol, contribute to the sedative effects of Valeriana officinalis teas, helping to calm the nervous system and alleviate bruxism symptoms. The benefits of using Valeriana officinalis teas to treat bruxism include reduced stress and anxiety, improved sleep quality, and a decrease in the frequency and severity of jaw clenching episodes.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh or dried Valeriana officinalis roots.
Use 1 tablespoon of Valeriana officinalis roots for every 1 cup of boiling water.
Place the Valeriana officinalis roots in a teapot or a heat-resistant cup.
Pour 1 cup of boiling water over the Valeriana officinalis roots. Let it steep for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea and drink it 30 minutes before bedtime to help with jaw clenching and relaxation.
preparations.preparation_study: According to "Current pharmaceutical biotechnology", Valeriana officinalis teas may be beneficial in treating anxiety disorders, which can potentially alleviate jaw clenching caused by anxiety.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Valeriana officinalis teas can be effective in treating jaw clenching, also known as bruxism, but may cause side effects such as drowsiness, stomach upset, and interactions with certain medications, including blood thinners and sedatives. To minimize potential side effects, take Valeriana officinalis teas in moderation, avoid taking it before driving or operating heavy machinery, and be cautious when combining it with other herbal preparations or substances that may also cause drowsiness.
preparation_usage: Drink 1 cup of Valeriana officinalis tea 2-3 times a day.
Start with 1 teaspoon of dried root per cup of boiling water.
Gradually increase to 2 teaspoons if jaw clenching persists.
Use tea for at least 2 weeks to observe noticeable improvements.
Consult a healthcare professional if jaw clenching continues.

preparations.herb_desc: Passiflora incarnata teas have been traditionally used to treat jaw clenching, also known as bruxism, due to their relaxing and calming properties. The bioactive constituents, including flavonoids, alkaloids, and glycosides, help to reduce muscle tension and anxiety that can lead to bruxism. By promoting relaxation and reducing stress, Passiflora incarnata teas help to prevent jaw clenching and grinding, allowing for a restful night's sleep. The benefits of using this herbal preparation to treat jaw clenching include improved sleep quality, reduced stress and anxiety, and a decrease in the risk of tooth wear and other related health issues.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh or dried Passiflora incarnata flowers.
Combine 1 cup of flowers with 1 cup of boiling water in a heat-resistant cup.
Steep the mixture for 5-7 minutes, then strain the liquid using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth.
Add 1 tablespoon of honey (optional) to the tea and mix well.
Drink 1/2 to 1 cup of the tea 2-3 times a day to help with jaw clenching.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Passiflora incarnata teas can be used to treat jaw clenching, also known as bruxism, due to its sedative properties, but possible side effects include drowsiness, dizziness, and interactions with other medications that affect the central nervous system. Precautions to take when using Passiflora incarnata teas include starting with a low dose, avoiding concurrent use with other sedatives or antidepressants, and being aware of potential allergic reactions or stomach upset.
preparation_usage: Drink 2 cups of Passiflora incarnata tea 3 times a day.
Start with 1 teaspoon of dried herb per cup of boiling water.
Gradually increase dose to 2 teaspoons per cup as needed.
Use tea for 2-3 weeks to see noticeable reduction in jaw clenching.
Consult a healthcare professional for continued treatment and monitoring.

preparations.herb_desc: Zingiber officinale teas, derived from the roots of the ginger plant, have been traditionally used to treat jaw clenching ailments, also known as bruxism. The bioactive constituents of this herbal preparation, including gingerols and shogaols, possess anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties that help to relax muscles and reduce pain in the jaw area. By reducing muscle tension and promoting relaxation, Zingiber officinale teas can help to alleviate the symptoms of bruxism, including jaw clenching and grinding. The benefits of using Zingiber officinale teas to treat bruxism include reduced pain and discomfort, improved sleep quality, and a decrease in stress and anxiety levels.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 teaspoon of dried Zingiber officinale root, 1 cup of boiling water, and a tea infuser or strainer.
Add the dried Zingiber officinale root to the tea infuser or strainer.
Pour the boiling water over the root and let it steep for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea into a cup using the infuser or strainer.
Drink the tea 2-3 times a day to help relieve jaw clenching.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Zingiber officinale teas can be used to treat jaw clenching ailment, but it may cause side effects such as stomach upset, dizziness, and allergic reactions in some individuals. To use Zingiber officinale teas safely, precautions include taking small amounts initially to assess tolerance, avoiding consumption before bedtime to prevent sleep disturbances, and being cautious with other medications or substances that may interact with it.
preparation_usage: Drink 1 cup of Zingiber officinale tea 2-3 times a day.
Start with 1/2 teaspoon of Zingiber officinale powder per cup.
Gradually increase to 1 teaspoon per cup if needed.
Use Zingiber officinale tea for 1-2 weeks to see noticeable relief.
Consult a doctor if jaw clenching persists after 2 weeks.

preparations.herb_desc: Lavandula angustifolia teas have been traditionally used to treat the jaw clenching ailment known as bruxism, due to their calming and relaxing properties. The bioactive constituents of this herbal preparation, including linalool and linalyl acetate, help to reduce muscle tension and promote a sense of calm, thereby alleviating the symptoms of bruxism. The benefits of Lavandula angustifolia teas in treating bruxism include improved sleep quality, reduced anxiety and stress levels, and a decrease in jaw pain and discomfort. Regular consumption of Lavandula angustifolia teas may also help to improve overall well-being and reduce the frequency and severity of bruxism episodes.
preparation_procedure: Gather 2 cups of fresh Lavandula angustifolia flowers or 1 cup of dried flowers.
Heat 1 cup of water in a kettle or pot.
Pour the hot water over the Lavandula angustifolia flowers in a large cup or teapot.
Let the mixture steep for 5-7 minutes. Strain the tea with a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth.
Drink 1 cup of the tea, 2-3 times a day, to help with jaw clenching. Store any leftover tea in the fridge for up to 24 hours.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Lavandula angustifolia teas can be used to treat jaw clenching, also known as bruxism, due to its calming effects on the nervous system, but possible side effects may include drowsiness, digestive upset, and allergic reactions in some individuals. Precautions should be taken when using Lavandula angustifolia teas to treat jaw clenching, such as starting with small doses, avoiding consumption before bedtime, and monitoring for any adverse reactions, especially in individuals with sensitive stomachs or allergies.
preparation_usage: Drink 1 cup of Lavandula angustifolia tea after breakfast.
Take 2 teaspoons of Lavandula angustifolia tea before bedtime.
Use 1 tablespoon of dried Lavandula angustifolia in tea.
Drink tea 2-3 times a day for best results.
Consult doctor for proper dosage and frequency.

preparations.herb_desc: Melissa officinalis teas have been traditionally used to treat jaw clenching, also known as bruxism, due to their calming and relaxing properties. The herbal preparation helps to alleviate this ailment by promoting relaxation of the facial muscles, reducing stress and anxiety, and improving sleep quality. The bioactive constituents, including flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, contribute to its therapeutic effects by acting as a natural anxiolytic and sedative. Regular consumption of Melissa officinalis teas has been found to provide benefits in treating jaw clenching, including reduced muscle tension, improved sleep patterns, and a decrease in overall stress levels.

Add 2 tablespoons of Melissa officinalis leaves to a cup.
Pour 1 cup of boiling water over the leaves.

Drink 1 cup of the tea 2-3 times a day to help with jaw clenching.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Melissa officinalis teas can be a helpful remedy for jaw clenching, but it may also cause side effects such as stomach upset, nausea, or diarrhea in some individuals due to its potential interactions with other medications. To use Melissa officinalis teas safely, it is recommended to start with a small dose and gradually increase as needed, avoid consuming it before bedtime or when engaging in activities that require focus, and be cautious if you have any underlying health conditions, such as low blood pressure or allergies.

Start with 1 teaspoon of dried herb per cup of boiling water

preparations.herb_desc: Glycyrrhiza glabra teas, also known as licorice root tea, have been traditionally used to treat the jaw clenching ailment known as temporomandibular joint disorder (TMJ). The anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties of this herbal preparation help to reduce muscle tension and alleviate pain in the jaw and facial area. The bioactive constituents, including glycyrrhizin and flavonoids, help to inhibit the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes and promote relaxation in the jaw muscles. Regular consumption of Glycyrrhiza glabra teas has been shown to provide relief from TMJ symptoms, such as jaw pain and clicking, and can also help to reduce stress and anxiety that may be contributing to the condition.
preparation_procedure: Get 1 cup of water and bring it to a boil.
Add 1-2 tablespoons of dried Glycyrrhiza glabra root to the boiling water.
Reduce heat to low and let it simmer for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea into a cup and discard the root.
Drink 1 cup of tea 2-3 times a day to help with jaw clenching.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Glycyrrhiza glabra teas can be used to treat jaw clenching ailments, but it may cause side effects such as increased blood pressure, low potassium levels, and changes in hormone levels due to its licorice root content. To use these teas safely, it is essential to consume them in moderation, avoid using them for extended periods, and be aware of any underlying kidney or heart conditions that may be exacerbated by the herbal preparation's potential effects.
preparation_usage: Drink 1 cup of Glycyrrhiza glabra tea 2 times a day.
Start with 1 tablespoon of dried root per cup, adjust to taste.
Steep for 5-7 minutes, then strain and discard the root.
Use tea for 2-4 weeks, then take a 1-week break if needed.
Monitor jaw clenching and adjust usage as needed with a healthcare expert.

preparations.herb_desc: Camellia sinensis teas, also known as green tea, have been traditionally used to help treat jaw clenching, also known as bruxism. The anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties of Camellia sinensis teas, particularly the high levels of catechins, help to reduce muscle tension and soothe the jaw muscles, alleviating symptoms of bruxism. preparation_procedure: Gather 1 teaspoon of Camellia sinensis leaves (black, green, or white) and 1 cup of boiling water.
Heat water to 95°C (203°F) for black tea, 70-80°C (158-176°F) for green tea, or 70-80°C (158-176°F) for white tea.
Steep the tea leaves in the boiling water for 3-5 minutes, depending on the tea type and desired strength.
Strain the tea into a cup and discard the tea leaves. Add 1-2 tablespoons of honey or lemon to taste, if desired.
Drink 1-2 cups of the tea per day, ideally 30 minutes before bedtime, to help relax and reduce jaw clenching.
preparations.preparation_study: According to Journal of ethnopharmacology, Camellia sinensis teas may not be directly linked to jaw clenching, as the study focuses on its use as an antiplaque agent and its antibiofilm properties in controlling microbial biofilms in the oral cavity, but it is worth noting that Camellia sinensis was found to be effective in reducing dental plaque and gingivitis.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Camellia sinensis teas can be used to treat jaw clenching ailments, but it may cause side effects such as drowsiness, stomach upset, and interactions with certain medications, particularly blood thinners, which can increase the risk of bleeding. When using Camellia sinensis teas to treat jaw clenching, it is recommended to start with small amounts, avoid consumption before bedtime, and monitor your body's sensitivity to the tea's caffeine content and other compounds.

To use Cimicifuga racemosa teas safely, it's recommended to start with small doses, gradually increasing the amount as needed, and to avoid consuming it in large quantities or for extended periods, as this may lead to over-sedation and interact with other medications.

preparations.herb_desc: Corydalis ambigua teas have been traditionally used to treat the jaw clenching ailment, also known as bruxism. This herbal preparation is said to help alleviate the symptoms of bruxism due to its sedative and relaxing properties, which help to calm the nervous system and reduce muscle tension. The bioactive constituents of Corydalis ambigua, including tetrahydropalmatine and tetrahydroberberine, are believed to contribute to its therapeutic effects by acting as GABA receptor agonists and potassium channel openers, which help to reduce muscle spasms and promote relaxation. The benefits of using Corydalis ambigua teas to treat bruxism include reduced jaw pain and discomfort, improved sleep quality, and a decrease in stress and anxiety levels.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of dried Corydalis ambigua roots.
Measure 2 tablespoons of dried Corydalis ambigua roots and add to a teapot.
Boil 1 cup of water and pour it over the dried Corydalis ambigua roots in the teapot.
Steep the mixture for 5-7 minutes, then strain the liquid.
Drink the tea 2-3 times a day to help alleviate jaw clenching.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Corydalis ambigua teas can be used to treat jaw clenching ailment, but possible side effects may include drowsiness, dizziness, and stomach upset, particularly when consumed in large quantities or combined with other medications. To use Corydalis ambigua teas safely, it's essential to start with a low dose, gradually increasing the amount as needed, and to avoid consuming them before driving or operating heavy machinery.

preparations.herb_desc: Thymus vulgaris teas, made from the leaves of the thyme plant, have been used for centuries to treat various health issues, including jaw clenching, also known as bruxism. The relaxing properties of thymus vulgaris teas, particularly its sedative and anxiolytic effects, help to calm the mind and body, reducing stress and anxiety that can lead to jaw clenching. The bioactive constituents of thymus vulgaris teas, including thymol, carvacrol, and rosmarinic acid, have been found to have a soothing effect on the muscles and nerves, helping to relax the jaw and surrounding muscles. By consuming thymus vulgaris teas, individuals can experience relief from jaw clenching and related symptoms, such as headaches and toothaches, while also benefiting from its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 tablespoon of dried Thymus vulgaris leaves.
Measure 1 cup of boiling water.
Pour the boiling water over the Thymus vulgaris leaves in a cup.
Let the mixture steep for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea and drink it slowly to help with jaw clenching.
preparations.preparation_study: According to Journal of cancer research and clinical oncology, Thymus vulgaris teas are not specifically mentioned for jaw clenching, but Thymus spp. is listed as one of the plants with good evidence for alleviating symptoms of oral mucositis, which may be related to jaw clenching due to the painful side effects of cancer treatment.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Thymus vulgaris teas can be used to treat jaw clenching, but possible side effects may include dizziness, nausea, and allergic reactions in some individuals, particularly those with sensitive skin or allergies. Precautions should be taken when using this herbal preparation, such as starting with small amounts, monitoring blood pressure, and avoiding consumption if pregnant or breastfeeding, as well as being cautious with interactions with other medications.

preparations.herb_desc: Cymbopogon citratus teas, also known as lemongrass tea, have been traditionally used to treat jaw clenching ailment, also known as bruxism, due to its calming and relaxing properties. The herbal preparation helps to treat this ailment by reducing muscle tension and promoting a good night's sleep, which can contribute to the development of bruxism. The bioactive constituents of Cymbopogon citratus tea, including citral, geraniol, and limonene, have been found to have anxiolytic and sedative effects, which can help to alleviate the symptoms of bruxism. Regular consumption of Cymbopogon citratus tea has been found to benefit individuals with bruxism by promoting relaxation, reducing stress and anxiety, and improving sleep quality, ultimately leading to a reduction in jaw clenching episodes.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh Cymbopogon citratus leaves (or 2 tablespoons of dried leaves) and 1 cup of boiling water.
Steep the leaves in boiling water for 5-7 minutes to release the oils and flavors.
Strain the mixture into a cup using a tea strainer or a piece of cheesecloth.
Add 1 tablespoon of honey (optional) to sweeten the tea, if desired.
Drink the tea 2-3 times a day to help relax the jaw and reduce clenching.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Cymbopogon citratus teas can be used to treat jaw clenching ailment, but possible side effects may include stomach upset, nausea, and allergic reactions such as skin rashes or itching. When using these teas, precautions should be taken to avoid overconsumption, which may lead to interactions with other medications or exacerbate underlying health conditions, especially for individuals with sensitive stomachs or allergies.

preparations.herb_desc: Origanum vulgare teas, also known as oregano tea, have been traditionally used to treat jaw clenching ailment, commonly referred to as bruxism. This herbal preparation helps to treat bruxism by its relaxing and calming effects on the nervous system, reducing muscle tension and promoting a sense of calmness. The bioactive constituents of Origanum vulgare tea, including carvacrol and thymol, contribute to its analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties, which help to alleviate pain and discomfort associated with bruxism. Regular consumption of Origanum vulgare tea has been shown to provide benefits in reducing the frequency and severity of bruxism episodes, promoting overall oral health and well-being.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 tablespoon of dried Origanum vulgare leaves and 1 cup of boiling water.
Steep the Origanum vulgare leaves in boiling water for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the mixture into a cup and discard the leaves.
Add 1 tablespoon of honey (optional) to the tea and mix well.
Drink the tea 2-3 times a day to help with jaw clenching and stress relief.
preparations.preparation_study: According to the study, Origanum vulgare teas are among the herbal remedies reported by Moroccan herbalists for treatment of various health issues, but there is no specific mention of jaw clenching treatment.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Origanum vulgare teas can be used to treat jaw clenching, but possible side effects include gastrointestinal upset, allergic reactions, and interactions with other medications, particularly blood thinners. To minimize these risks, it's essential to start with a low dose, gradually increase as needed, and avoid combining with other herbal preparations or medications that may exacerbate potential side effects.
